evals.query: In my Kora Java service I need to map CreateOrderDto to an Order entity. Generate a UUID for the id and the current timestamp for createdAt on conversion. Set me up.
expected_behavior: Adds annotationProcessor "org.mapstruct:mapstruct-processor:1.5.5.Final" and implementation "org.mapstruct:mapstruct:1.5.5.Final" alongside the mandatory annotationProcessor "ru.tinkoff.kora:annotation-processors"
Defines an @Mapper interface with a toEntity method using @Mapping(target = "id", expression = "java(java.util.UUID.randomUUID())") and an expression for createdAt
Explains the mapper is injected by constructor because Kora's MapStruct extension registers the generated *MapperImpl as a component
Does not put @Component on the mapper and injects via constructor rather than field injection

evals.query: My Car entity has numberOfSeats but my CarTO DTO has seatCount. How do I map between them with MapStruct in Kora?
expected_behavior: Uses @Mapping(source = "numberOfSeats", target = "seatCount") on an @Mapper interface method
Notes that Kora discovers the @Mapper interface automatically and wires the generated CarMapperImpl into the DI graph
Does not require any *Module to be plugged into @KoraApp for MapStruct
Injects the mapper by constructor and uses @Tag only if disambiguation is needed, not field-injection annotations from other frameworks

evals.query: I want a PATCH endpoint that updates an existing Order from PatchOrderDto. Only non-null DTO fields should overwrite the entity, and id and createdAt must never change.
expected_behavior: Declares a void applyPatch(@MappingTarget Order existing, PatchOrderDto patch) method
Uses @BeanMapping(nullValuePropertyMappingStrategy = NullValuePropertyMappingStrategy.IGNORE) for PATCH semantics
Marks id and createdAt with @Mapping(target = ..., ignore = true)
Does not create a new entity instance; uses Kora's @HttpController/@HttpRoute for the endpoint rather than annotations from other web frameworks

evals.query: My Order entity has a status of type OrderStatus enum, but OrderDto needs status as a lowercase String. How do I convert it with MapStruct?
expected_behavior: Defines a @Named("statusToString") helper that lowercases the enum name
References it from the mapping via @Mapping(source = "status", target = "status", qualifiedByName = "statusToString")
Keeps the mapper as an @Mapper interface (or abstract class for stateful helpers) with no @Component
Does not invent a Kora-specific enum-mapping annotation

evals.query: Can I use MapStruct in my Kotlin Kora project that already uses KSP? What's the Gradle config and the catch?
expected_behavior: Explains MapStruct on Kotlin needs kapt, which collides with Kora's ksp "ru.tinkoff.kora:symbol-processors"
Pins Kotlin 1.9.10 and KSP 1.9.10-1.0.13 with allowSourcesFromOtherPlugins = true and KspTask dependsOn the kapt tasks
Warns the first clean build may fail and to run the build a second time, and notes the kora-kotlin-crud example hand-writes mappers instead
Does not claim KSP processes MapStruct directly and relies on Kora's compile-time component wiring rather than runtime component scanning

evals.query: When mapping to my Order entity, status should default to PENDING when the source is null, and a type field should always be INTERNAL regardless of input.
expected_behavior: Uses @Mapping(target = "status", defaultValue = "PENDING") so the value applies only when the source is null
Uses @Mapping(target = "type", constant = "INTERNAL") which ignores the source entirely
Explains the difference between defaultValue and constant
Keeps the mapper as an @Mapper interface without @Component and uses constructor injection, not field injection

evals.query: My Kora build floods warnings about unmapped target properties on my MapStruct mapper. How do I silence them cleanly?
expected_behavior: Adds @Mapper(unmappedTargetPolicy = ReportingPolicy.IGNORE) as used in the kora-java-crud PetMapper
Mentions the alternative of explicitly mapping or ignoring each target field
Confirms this does not affect how Kora registers the generated *MapperImpl as a component
Does not disable the Kora annotation processor and keeps the fix to the @Mapper annotation

evals.query: I only have two DTO-to-entity conversions in my Kora service. Should I add MapStruct?
expected_behavior: Recommends skipping MapStruct for 1-3 simple mappers and writing a plain constructor mapping instead
Explains MapStruct pays off with many DTO/entity pairs and high field overlap with compile-time verification
If MapStruct is still chosen, uses @Mapper with no @Component and the MapStruct annotationProcessor
Does not pull in MapStruct or any unnecessary *Module just for two trivial conversions
